location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

seniority.level: Group PM / Director-level IC or people manager
level_signals: 6+ years of experience in product management
2+ years of experience managing product managers
Leads a team of PMs across three verticals
Comp range $176K-$220K base consistent with GPM/Director level at Lyft

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of experience in product management, product design, or equivalent consulting experience with significant time spent leading consumer mobile apps or marketplace products
must_haves.qualifier: consumer mobile apps or marketplace products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 2+ years of experience managing product managers
Manage and grow a team of product managers, providing development, mentorship, and coaching to help them achieve their goals and Lyft's goals
Set an ambitious vision for the Verticals team that motivates your team, peers, and leadership — and that accounts for the distinct dynamics of airports, scheduled rides, and events
Strong ability to define and analyze metrics that inform the success of products and the health of the business
Lead and align cross-functional partners including engineers, designers, data scientists, analysts, marketers, business partners, and operations to drive a shared vision
Strong communication skills and intuition for communication strategy at every level — within teams, with partner teams, and to executive leadership
Completely own your team's products — existing and new — from ideation through development, experimentation, launch, growth, and maintenance, caring deeply about both rider and driver experiences
Natural ability to make things happen around you — you manage project ambiguity, complexity, and interdependencies in an organized and structured way
Detail oriented with superior organizational skills — balancing multiple projects, deadlines, and requests should be second nature to you
Passion for Lyft and what we are trying to achieve in sustainable urban transportation

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience working with or alongside data science and operations teams on complex, products; familiarity with experimentation, marketplace dynamics, or logistics is a plus

scope.team_size_estimate: multiple PMs (team of PMs across three verticals)
scope.surface: Airports, scheduled rides, and events verticals on the Lyft platform
scope.business_model: marketplace / rideshare platform (B2C, two-sided rider-driver marketplace)
primary_metrics_inferred: Rider booking conversion and satisfaction across verticals
Growth within airport, scheduled rides, and events verticals
Rider-driver matching efficiency in high-variability environments
core_responsibilities: Manage and grow a team of product managers, providing development, mentorship, and coaching
Set an ambitious vision for the Verticals team covering airports, scheduled rides, and events
Understand Lyft's strategic and competitive position; set team goals and success metrics
Own team products end-to-end from ideation through launch, growth, and maintenance
Lead and align cross-functional partners to drive a shared vision
Communicate roadmaps, priorities, experiments, and decisions to partner teams and executive leadership
Optimize rider-driver matching, demand forecasting, real-time driver guidance, and targeted incentives

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($176K-$220K base) plus equity and bonus
Clear product surface with three well-defined verticals
High visibility role with explicit executive exposure
Hybrid with flexibility to work remotely up to 4 weeks per year
Comprehensive benefits package including 18 weeks paid parental leave
Encourages broad backgrounds (PM, product design, or consulting)
red_flags: Strictly San Francisco-based with 3 required in-office days per week (Mon/Wed/Thu) — not suitable for remote candidates
Posting first seen / last updated dates not available — unclear how long role has been open

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required, people management scope, and comp range)
scope.team_size_estimate (inferred from 'team of product managers' phrasing; exact count not stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product and company description)
ambiguities: The JD states '6+ years of experience in product management, product design, or equivalent consulting experience' — it is unclear whether consulting experience fully substitutes for product management or is intended as partial equivalency. Treated as hard requirement with consulting as an acknowledged alternative path.
The 'data science and operations' nice-to-have bullet contains a grammatical artifact ('complex, products') suggesting possible editing artifact; interpreted as 'complex products'.
